perturbation.original_text: 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party or vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person,
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 In this Agreement, including the recitals, except as expressly provided or unless the context otherwise requires, (a) Affiliate means, in respect of a party hereto, a corporation which is the subsidiary of the party, but not vice versa or where each of the party and the corporation is controlled by the same person,
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the definition of 'Affiliate' to exclude the 'vice versa' relationship (i.e., parent company not being an affiliate of its subsidiary) creates an in-text contradiction. Section 1.3 states that the agreements extend to all subsidiaries and affiliates, using the names of Glamis and Western Copper to refer collectively to themselves and their affiliates. By restricting the affiliate definition, this section is contradicted because Section 1.3 no longer binds the parent company through the actions of the subsidiary.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 Bach of the parties hereto agree that all of their covenants, agreements and obligations hereunder shall extend to and be binding upon and may be enforced against any and all of their respective subsidiaries and other Affiliates, as well as against the parties themselves, as the case may be, and that the names of Glamis and Western Copper will herein be deemed to refer collectively to Glamis and all of its subsidiaries and other Affiliates and to Western Copper and all of its subsidiaries and other Affiliates, respectively.
perturbation.justification: The modification to the definition of 'Affiliate' in Section 1.1, specifically excluding the 'vice versa' relationship (parent company not being an affiliate of its subsidiary), directly contradicts Section 1.3. Section 1.3 states that all obligations extend to subsidiaries and affiliates and that the names Glamis and Western Copper refer collectively to the entities and their affiliates. By narrowing the definition of 'Affiliate,' Section 1.1 weakens the binding nature of Section 1.3, creating a conflict in the contractual obligations. Specifically, Section 1.3 would no longer bind the parent company through the actions of the subsidiary, as the definition of affiliate in Section 1.1 no longer includes the parent.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will not include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.explanation: By negating the inclusion of 'principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor' in the non-compete clause, this change introduces an in-text contradiction within Section 2.1 itself. Originally, it prohibited Western Copper from engaging in Mining Activities 'in any manner whatsoever,' including as a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor. The modified text now implies that Western Copper *can* act as a principal, agent, shareholder or advisor for a Person engaging in Mining Activities, which is a direct contradiction of the 'in any manner whatsoever' clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1 Western Copper covenants and agrees with Glamis that, for a period of 2 years after the Effective Date, it will not, directly or indirectly, either individually or in partnership or jointly or in conjunction with any Person, which will not include being a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor of such Person or in any other manner whatsoever, (a) carry on or be engaged in Mining Activities, or i 162967.3 -4- (b) advise, lend money to, guarantee the debts or obligations of or permit its name to be used by any Person who carries on or is engaged in Mining Activities, in the Area of Non-Competition.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 2.1 negates the inclusion of 'principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor' in the non-compete clause, directly contradicting the 'in any manner whatsoever' clause within the same section. The original text prevented Western Copper from engaging in Mining Activities 'in any manner whatsoever,' which included acting as a principal, agent, shareholder, or advisor. The modified text now implies that Western Copper can act in those roles, which creates a direct conflict with the original intention and overall scope of Section 2.1's non-compete agreement. The contradiction is significant because it alters the enforceability and scope of the non-compete agreement, rendering it unclear whether Western Copper can participate in Mining Activities through those roles.
